predictions.reasoning: Participant 310 (age 60, male, low depression/anxiety, very low extraversion, high trait PA) had a quiet indoor day with 51 min walking in 3 deliberate episodes — a positive physical activity signal for a cancer survivor. Minimal keyboard use (68 chars) is consistent with his introverted profile rather than social withdrawal distress. No driving beyond a brief 9-min trip suggests a relaxed home day. Baseline history shows a resilient, gratitude-oriented individual with strong family support whose emotional trajectory improves from mid-March onward. Evening EMA at 8:14 PM with indoor environment: person is home and winding down. No behavioral red flags — moderate positive affect, very low negative affect expected. Low ER desire given low distress profile and calm day.

Massively elevated walking (105 min, z=+10 vs baseline 6.7 min) + outdoor environment + driving = very active community/mobility day. Communication-dominant app use (8/13 min) and brief screen checks (17 sec/open avg) signal purposeful social engagement, not avoidance. Most similar day (2024-03-15, sim=0.99) yielded ER_desire=0. High trait PA (33), low trait NA (14), minimal clinical symptoms (PHQ=4, GAD=3) support a positive baseline. Historical narrative shows this person is family/community-active with gradual mood improvement starting mid-March. Active outdoor day inconsistent with pain or distress. Low ER desire predicted given activity level and prior-day precedent.

Today shows unusually elevated walking (60min, Z=+8.22) and screen use (55min, Z=+5.41) in an outdoor context, suggesting a more active/engaged day than typical for this person. Some communication app usage (12min) indicates mild social contact. Profile: low depression/anxiety, low trait NA, high social support — stable emotional baseline. Baseline history places this in the early-study stress/fatigue phase but gradual improvement is ongoing. Both behaviorally similar days (03-15: family dinner, 03-16: nap) show ER_desire=0, available=yes → non-receptive. Predicting moderate-positive affect above personal baseline, low NA, and consistent non-receptivity pattern.

Morning EMA at 9:37 AM. Walking dramatically elevated (z+64.67 vs morning baseline) combined with outdoor environment and 35-min drive suggests active, purposeful start to the day. Communication apps dominant (12/25 min screen) with 1564 keyboard chars indicates social engagement. All 3 prior EMAs show ER_desire=0 and available=yes; all 3 most similar historical days match this exact profile (similarity 0.77–0.87) with consistent non-receptivity. High trait PA (33), low depression/anxiety (PHQ-8=4, GAD-7=3) and profile narrative showing improving trajectory through March supports positive state. Elevated walking argues against pain despite Stage 5 cancer. Confidence slightly tempered by morning EMA timing (limited day-level data) and device gap in hourly queries.

Morning EMA revealed work stress and rumination. By afternoon, behavioral signals show a significant shift: elevated walking (7 episodes, z=+4.18), outdoor environment, and communication app dominance (12/25 min screen) suggest active coping. Frequent brief screen checks (67 opens, 25 min total, z=+9.78) indicate residual restlessness but not disengagement. Three most similar past days all show ER_desire=0, INT_availability=yes — extremely strong prior that holds. Low trait NA (14) and moderate PA (33) anchor the emotional range. Worried/forecasting states remain true due to morning work concerns persisting into afternoon, but positive coping behaviors (outdoor activity, social communication) support moderate PA and available status.

Participant 310 shows unusually high walking activity today (62min, z=+9.24 above evening baseline), outdoor exposure, and communication-dominant app usage — collectively indicating a physically and socially active, positive-leaning day. The afternoon diary confirmed sunny weather and outdoor time. Morning work concerns appear resolved by evening. Most critically, this person has shown ER_desire=0 on every single EMA today (3 prior) and all similar past days (0/3 receptive), making zero ER desire near-certain. High trait PA (33), low trait NA (14), low PHQ/GAD scores support moderate positive and low negative affect predictions.

Morning EMA (8:53 AM) for participant with consistent zero ER_desire across all 6 prior entries — strongest signal. Outdoor environment, 52 min walking, 31 min driving, and communication-dominant screen use (19/28 min) indicate an active, engaged morning. Trait profile (PA=33, NA=14) anchors toward positive affect. Recent trajectory shows improving mood. All 4 most similar days show ER_desire=0, INT_availability=yes, receptive=no. Some mild worry predicted consistent with prior morning pattern (3/18: work concerns going through mind). PA elevated above personal baseline; NA below baseline. Not cheerful at peak given early morning timing, but pleased, grateful, and positively engaged.

Person 310 is having an unusually active outdoor afternoon: walking 52 min (Z=+3.46 above personal norm), communication apps dominating 19/28 min of screen time, outdoor environment. Morning EMA was relaxed ('Just woke up. Ha.'). All 7 prior EMAs show ER_desire=0 — a near-certain anchor. Similar days (similarity 0.91–0.99) unanimously show ER_desire=0. High trait PA (33) and low trait NA (14) anchor expected state range. Afternoon context (active, social, outdoor) matches 03-18 14:02 profile where he was available. No work-stress or fatigue signals present. Predicted low NA, moderate-high PA, no ER desire, available for afternoon interaction.

Participant 310 shows a consistently positive behavioral profile today: outdoor activity, 6 walking episodes (52 min), communication-dominated app use (68%), and many quick screen checks suggesting social responsiveness rather than anxious scrolling. Earlier EMA at 14:00 confirms outdoor lunch outing — a positive routine activity. All 8 prior observations show ER_desire=0 with no exceptions. High trait PA (33) and low NA (14) set a strong positive baseline. Today's active, socially-connected day with no stress signals (unlike yesterday's to-do-list stress) points to a calm, content evening. Similar days (0.91–0.99 similarity) all show ER_desire=0 and mostly INT_availability=yes. Predicting positive affect with minimal negative affect and full availability.

Morning EMA at 09:05. Behavioral profile: stationary overnight (sleep), then unusually elevated walking (z=+19.78 above morning baseline) and outdoor context suggest an active morning routine or commute. Screen use is quick-check pattern (62 opens / 7min total). Low communication so far. No stress indicators in passive data. Trait profile (PA=33, NA=14) combined with consistent ER_desire=0 across all 9 prior observations and 4 behaviorally-similar days all showing available=yes, receptive=no strongly anchors prediction. Outdoor physical activity slightly elevates positive affect prediction above baseline. Confidence tempered by morning timing — day has barely started.

Morning EMA showed ER_desire=7 with poor sleep, car taken by child, heavy workload — highest distress observed for this participant. By afternoon, 1h13m driving suggests car situation resolved and he reached his destination. However, 62 screen opens in only 7m (avg ~7s each) is a strong anxious-checking signature and significantly elevated vs his baseline (Z=+3.25). Low comm app usage (2m) indicates minimal social support engagement. Outdoor environment is mildly positive and consistent with his lunch-break pattern. Similar behavioral days had ER_desire=0, but today's morning context meaningfully elevates NA and ER_desire above his typical afternoon pattern. His high trait PA (33) and low trait NA (14) buffer against severe negative states; afternoon likely shows partial recovery from morning peak but residual worry/stress given ongoing work pressures. INT_availability=yes consistent with all similar days and his typical afternoon openness.

Morning EMA after two consecutive nights of poor sleep and elevated stress (ER_desire 7→3 yesterday). The 63 rapid phone screen checks (10m total, ~9.5s avg) strongly signal anxious/restless morning behavior — z=+4.24 above his typical morning. Elevated walking (35m, 5 episodes) is unusual for his morning baseline (z=+39.75), possibly morning restlessness or commute activity. Most similar past day (3-20, similarity=1.00) featured 'Tired today, didn't sleep well' with ER_desire=3, INT=no. Last 4 EMAs all show INT_availability=no; no basis to predict change. Moderate NA expected given ongoing fatigue/stress cycle; PA below personal norm due to sleep deprivation. ER_desire likely ~4: still elevated above baseline but coming down from prior peak. Intervention availability predicted NO based on strong recent pattern.

Afternoon EMA follows a clear 2-day fatigue chain: poor sleep 3/20 (ER=7 morning → ER=3 afternoon) and poor sleep 3/21 (ER=1 morning, 'super tired'). Screen behavior (63 opens, 10min total) reflects restless phone-checking without engagement — classic fatigue/distraction signature. Walking unusually elevated vs baseline (Z=+2.69) and screen time elevated (Z=+5.57), suggesting some arousal/checking but low productivity. Most similar past day (3/20, similarity=1.0) was tired + unavailable (ER=3, no). All 5 similar days non-receptive. Given low trait NA (14), PHQ=4, and his generally resilient profile, NA kept modest. His consistent gratitude patterns and strong family social context maintain interactions quality above baseline despite tiredness. ER_desire=2 slightly above his personal mean (~1.1). INT_availability=no — fatigue pattern dominates, same as morning and yesterday.

P310 had a difficult morning (tired, poor sleep) but showed clear recovery: napped at lunch and was concentrating on work by afternoon with ER_desire=0. Evening behavioral profile shows substantially elevated walking (35min, Z=+3.73 vs his norm of 3.5min), suggesting an active evening outing consistent with family engagement (his diary baseline shows dinner/family as a recurring evening theme). Low screen time (10min) with frequent quick checks indicates relaxed monitoring, not anxiety. Minimal keyboard input suggests he's not working or stressed. Recovery arc (ER_desire 7→3→1→0 across 5 EMAs) strongly points to near-zero ER_desire tonight. Most similar past days (Mar 15, 17) show ER=0 with family social context. His low trait NA and effective nap-based coping yield moderate positive affect. INT_availability predicted yes (typical evening pattern for this user), though historically he is never truly receptive.

Morning EMA after positive social evening (church dinner, ER_desire=0). Three-day fatigue/stress arc (poor sleep, high ER_desire Mar 20-21) resolved by social rebound Thursday evening. Device off pre-EMA suggests commute/work transition. Morning drive (18 min) confirms work day start. All 5 similar behavioral days showed non-receptive pattern. High trait PA (33), low depression/anxiety (PHQ-8=4, GAD-7=3) support stable positive baseline. Morning EMAs historically show INT_availability=no (3/4 prior mornings). Low ER_desire expected given prior evening's positive context.

Morning diary confirmed good sleep and sunny weather (ER_desire=0). Afternoon shows significantly elevated walking (41 min, Z=+3.26) in outdoor environment, consistent with enjoying sunshine. Low screen engagement (brief checks only) and minimal communication fit his introverted profile. No fatigue/stress signals. High trait PA + good sleep trajectory → moderately elevated positive affect. Similar days show 0/5 receptive; his persistent work-hour unavailability pattern (ground truth ~no most afternoons) guides INT_availability=no.

Today is a consistently positive day for P310. Morning diary confirmed good sleep and sunshine; afternoon confirmed focused work productivity. Behavioral signals reinforce this: outdoor environment, walking 41 min (Z=+4.82, top 1% of his own history — strong positive activity signal), low but purposeful screen engagement (60 brief checks, only 9 min total suggesting calm). Both earlier EMAs today showed ER_desire=0. Walking elevation is the most striking anomaly — likely pleasant outdoor activity in good weather. Low keyboard/comm app use (1 min) suggests minimal social demands. This person has low extraversion, high trait PA, low trait NA, minimal depression/anxiety. The 5 most behaviorally similar days all showed non-receptivity (0/5 receptive) — historical evening availability is consistently 'no' regardless of emotional valence. Predicting a moderately positive evening with zero emotional regulation desire and continued unavailability.

Morning EMA at 9:19 AM. Participant shows active outdoor day with elevated walking (72 min, Z=+87.88 vs baseline) and driving (113 min), suggesting mobile engagement. Screen usage (10 min/55 opens) elevated vs morning baseline but still brief overall. Communication apps dominant (6/10 min), indicating brief social contact. All 5 behaviorally similar days showed ER_desire=0-3 with 0/5 receptive — strong non-receptivity pattern. Morning EMAs historically always INT_availability=no for this person. No behavioral signals of distress or elevated negative affect. Predicting moderate-positive state consistent with this participant's typical high-functioning baseline.

Saturday with strong morning self-report ('I just feel good', slept well, sunny). Extremely elevated walking (Z=+9.36, 72 min vs 2.8 typical) and 1h53m driving indicate active outdoor day. Very low screen time (10 min) confirms physical engagement over phone use. Communication apps (6 min) suggest some social contact. Sunday morning at home following a strong positive Saturday. Participant 310 has low trait depression/anxiety, high trait PA, and consistently shows ER_desire=0 throughout the study. No sleep disturbance signals, no work-related stress (weekend), and the trajectory from Saturday's diary entries ('I just feel good', 'nice dinner') supports a stable, mildly positive emotional state. Low social activity in the morning is typical for this highly introverted person. Five most similar past days all show ER_desire=0 and INT_availability=no, reinforcing prediction of non-receptivity.

Sunday afternoon for a well-rested participant (morning diary: 'well rested, not rushed').
